title: Kubernetes
objectives: Deploy an app with rolling updates
Reason about scaling, health and storage

sections.markdown: Kubernetes (K8s) is a container orchestrator: you declare the desired state (which apps, how many replicas, how they're networked), and controllers converge the cluster toward it — self-healing pods, rolling updates, autoscaling included.

rows: One (or few) containers sharing net/vol — the smallest unit
Stable virtual IP/DNS for a set of Pods
L7 HTTP routing into the cluster
Non-secret / secret config injected into Pods

sections.markdown: Understand the difference between **liveness** (restart the pod if wedged) and **readiness** (remove from load balancer if not ready). Miss readiness → users see 500s during deploys. Also: set CPU/memory requests correctly or the scheduler misplaces pods.

Managing raw YAML by hand across environments. Use **Helm** or **Kustomize** to templatize and vary per env; commit manifests to git (GitOps with Argo CD/Flux) so cluster state is version-controlled and auditable.

One container concern per Pod; expose via Service; keep secrets in a manager (Vault/SOPS/Sealed Secrets) — not raw K8s Secrets; add HPA for stateless workloads; use PodDisruptionBudget for HA during upgrades.

flashcards.back: Pod = runtime unit; Deployment = declarative replicas + rollouts; Service = stable network endpoint.
Liveness restarts a wedged pod; readiness removes an unready pod from the Service load balancer.

flashcards.front: How do you run a stateful DB on K8s?
flashcards.back: StatefulSet + PersistentVolumeClaim for stable identity and storage.

quiz.q: The smallest deployable unit is a…
quiz.explain: A Pod is one (or few) containers sharing net/vol.

quiz.q: To do a zero-downtime rollout you rely on…
quiz.explain: Readiness prevents traffic to not-ready pods during rollout.
Templated + version-controlled manifests keep envs sane.

challenges.text: Deploy an app to a local cluster (kind/minikube) with a Deployment + Service.
Add readiness/liveness probes and a rolling update.
Add HPA scaling on CPU + a PodDisruptionBudget.
Deploy via Argo CD from a Git repo with Kustomize overlays per env.

interviewQuestions: What is Kubernetes and what problem does it solve?
Pod vs Deployment vs Service vs StatefulSet?
How do you keep secrets safe on K8s?
What is GitOps and why use it?
